forecaster_note: Slow fade in size as the S/SE swell backs off — surf still head to overhead at many breaks, with the S-magnets holding 2x-OH. SW to maybe W winds in the morning for the cleanest conditions (southern ends tidiest, but more widespread than the past couple of days), then light-to-moderate S'lies into the afternoon add bump to all but the south corners. Fri: exposures cleaning up as the SE swell eases and winds go W — good surf on the back end. Weekend: Sat offshore morning window for good clean surf, Sun small but crisp with all-day offshores. INCOMING: the next solid S/SE fills in from Wednesday for another round of decent to maybe solid size. — Hugh McDowell (Surfline)

wind.summary: SW 10kph (20 gusts) early — offshore and clean through the morning, best of the week so far. Light-to-moderate S'ly fills in this arvo with bump for everything except the south corners.

shark.summary: No shark detections anywhere on the Northern Beaches (Palm Beach → Manly) in the past 24h — drumline, drone and SLS feeds all quiet. Only notice: no SMART drumlines deployed on the Northern Beaches South stretch (Fairy Bower–Turimetta) yesterday, so drumline coverage there is reduced. Usual drill — scan the lineup, surf with company.

days.label: Today
days.note: The swell is easing but the wind finally plays ball — SW offshore through the morning before an arvo S'ly. Dee Why fair-to-good at 3–4ft+ with the SE 139° angle suiting the bank (not the dead-S wrap), the reefs still 2x-OH for the committed. Low tide 8:58am means your 10–12 window rides the incoming push — clean, consistent (100/100) and rippable. Best morning of the week to date.

dee-why.window: The pick — SE 139° is a workable angle here and the SW wind is straight offshore. Clean overhead surf, 100/100 consistency. Mid-morning on the incoming after the 8:58am low is your slot; the Point for frontside rights.

tip.label: Green light
tip.text: 439kJ with 100/100 consistency and real offshore wind — the shape day after the raw size. SE 139° hits the bank properly, not the dead-S wrap. Surf your 10–12 window on the push and grab the Point rights before the arvo S'ly.

long-reef.window: Biggest of the four — 4–6ft, 570kJ onto the outer reefs with the SW sitting cross-shore. More organised than yesterday; still step-up territory for the morning only.

tip.label: Size with a catch
tip.text: 570kJ and 2x-overhead sets, but the SW runs cross-shore across the reefs. Rideable and cleaner than yesterday — if you want the size, dawn-to-mid-morning with the step-up. Otherwise Dee Why has the shape.

curl-curl.window: S-magnet still loaded at 4–5ft (447kJ), SW cross-shore. The south-end rights off the rocks are the tidy corner — cleanest before the arvo S'ly.

tip.label: South corner rights
tip.text: Curly holds 4–5ft of this fading swell at full 100/100 consistency. The open beach is workable in the SW but the south-corner rights (your frontside) are the quality option this morning.

manly.window: Clean, offshore and user-friendly — 2–3ft+ at 94/100 consistency. The mellow performance session if overhead reefs aren't the vibe today.

tip.label: Clean and easy
tip.text: North Steyne is fair-to-good with straight offshore SW — 2–3ft+ of clean, punchy peaks. The relaxed alternative: same clean window, half the consequence of the reefs.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: Exposures clean up as the SE swell eases and winds swing W — Surfline calls good surf on the back end of the day. Long Reef still 4–5ft, Curl Curl 2–4ft, Manly 2–3ft. Offshore all day means you're not tied to dawn; a late-morning or arvo hit could be the score.

dee-why.window: Easing to head-high with W offshores all day — clean, rippable daily-driver surf. Surfline flags the back end of the day as the good window.
alerts: ⚠️ Dee Why daily size table unavailable this refresh — sized from surrounding spots

long-reef.window: The size pick — 4–5ft holding on the reefs with W winds grooming it. Cleanest surf of the week out here; good on the back end.

curl-curl.window: 2–4ft and cleaning up with the W'ly — the south corner keeps the best shape as it fades.

manly.window: Clean 2–3ft — fun performance surf, offshore all day.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: The SE energy fades into the weekend but the winds are all-time — offshore Saturday morning, offshore ALL day Sunday. Sat: clean 2–3ft (reefs 2–4ft) in the morning window, your kind of fun-size session. Sun: small (1–2ft) but crisp and glassy — groveller or longboard paddle. Sat mid-morning is the call. Next solid S/SE arrives from Wednesday, and it looks big.

dee-why.window: Sat: clean 2–3ft in the offshore morning — punchy-beachie fun. Sun 1–2ft, grovel territory.
alerts: ⚠️ Dee Why daily size table unavailable this refresh — sized from surrounding spots

long-reef.window: Sat: 2–4ft of residual S energy, cleanest early. Sun eases to 1–2ft+.

curl-curl.window: Sat: 2–3ft clean in the morning window. Sun small (1–2ft+), all-day offshore.

manly.window: Sat: mellow clean 2–3ft. Sun 1–2ft — longboard or groveller only.
